ops.title: capt-hook `--hooks test` loads the REGISTERED pack from cache not the tree; @latest=release tag; mcp_active-gated pack messages make inline patterns mode-dependent
ops.body:
Hard-won mechanics from cc-notes task 4e0b9ac (the "5 codified rows fail on warn-message drift" pack fix).

- **`uvx capt-hook --hooks <dir> test` tests the REGISTERED pack from cache, not the working tree.** When a pack is registered in `.claude/hooks/packs.toml` (e.g. cc-notes → `github:yasyf/cc-notes@latest`), capt-hook loads its hooks by name from the content-addressed cache `~/.cache/captain-hook/packs/<name>@<sha>`, resolved from its source — even inside the pack's OWN repo. Editing `plugin/hooks/record.py` and re-running the test shows the OLD cached pack. Working-tree edits only take effect after commit+push+re-resolve. The cc-notes README says to verify with `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks test`, but that's the cache too. To get a real pre-push signal, copy the hooks dir to /tmp and RENAME the pack in `capt-hook.toml` (`name = "…-verify"`) so it doesn't shadow the cached one — but note /tmp is non-MCP (see below).

- **packs.toml `@latest` = newest RELEASE TAG, not the branch tip.** cc-notes pin cached as `latest@9e8fb84` = tag v0.27.0; newest was v0.28.0. `pack update <name>` re-resolves @latest to the newest release. Landing a pack fix in a consumer (cc-context 312/312) needs a NEW release of the pack repo, not just a pushed commit to main.

- **cc-notes pack warn messages are gated on `mcp_active(evt)` (plugin/hooks/common.py:270).** MCP-form ("log_add tool", "attach param", "task_add tool") when a cc-notes MCP liveness marker sits under the repo's git dir; CLI-form ("--attach", "cc-notes log add", "cc-notes task add") otherwise. CI's `hook-tests` job runs non-MCP (no marker) → CLI-form; a dev shell with the MCP running → MCP-form. An inline `tests={Input: Warn(pattern=…)}` row has ONE static pattern, so a mode-specific pattern passes in one env and fails the other — this is why the "drift" shows only in a dev shell yet CI is green, and why the naive MCP-form fix would BREAK CI. Fix pack-message drift with a MODE-AGNOSTIC fragment common to both branches (precedent: `record.py:632` already uses `Warn(pattern="attach")`).

- Editing files in cc-notes' shared jj working copy `@` while a concurrent session works there got my edit auto-snapshotted into their commit and swept away by them ("fix(hooks): drop record.py test-expectation WIP swept into the rewire commit"). Isolate cross-session pack edits with `jj workspace add -r main` — see [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]].

Related: [[capt-hook-cc-context-wiring]], [[capt-hook-local-testing]], [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]].
